Module 1: 	Positioning
!
Executive Summary
Positioning is how the market sees you. What do you look like from their
perspective?
Here’s an example: Toyota and Lexus are the same company. Toyota
appeals to the everyday consumer/driver, but they decided they wanted to
create a new luxury brand. Instead of repositioning their existing brand,
they created a new premium luxury brand - Lexus.
Your market is the same - people will always want to position you in the
low, middle, or high end. You need and want to be seen in the high-end of
your market as a leader that provides massive value.
A strongly executed product launch instantly positions you as a guru and
expert, that’s why PLF is so powerful!
Benefits of strong positioning
• 	Deal flow: opportunities to partner with other businesses
• 	Speaking engagements
• 	Joint Venture deals
• 	Charging high prices for your products and having higher margins
!

!
!
Everything you do in your business either builds or degrades your
business. That’s why it is so key to be aware of all your actions. Ultimately,
your positioning builds more positioning, and this allows you to go from
launching a single product to building a valuable business.
!
